content:
# Acts Chapter 8 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 234-235 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_008_page_001.jpg, ACT_008_page_002.jpg

---

**1.** And Saul was consenting to the killing of him. And there came-about in that day a great persecution upon the assembly, the [one] in Hierosolyma; and all were scattered throughout the regions of Ioudaia and Samareia, except the apostles.

**2.** And devout men carried-together Stephanos and made great lamentation over him.

**3.** But Saul was ravaging the assembly, going-into house after house, dragging both men and women, he was delivering [them] into prison.

**4.** The [ones] therefore having-been-scattered went-through [the land] proclaiming-good-news [with] the word.

**5.** And Philippos, having gone down to the city of Samareia, was proclaiming to them the Christ.

**6.** And the crowds were giving-heed with-one-accord to the [things] being spoken by Philippos, in their hearing and seeing the signs which he was doing.

**7.** For many of those having unclean spirits, crying-out [with] a great voice, they were coming-out; and many having-been-paralyzed and lame were healed.

**8.** And there came-about great joy in that city.

**9.** But a certain man, Simon by name, was previously-existing in the city practicing-magic and astonishing the nation of Samareia, saying himself to be someone great.

**10.** To whom all were giving-heed, from small unto great, saying, "This [one] is the Power of God, the [one] being called Great."

**11.** And they were giving-heed to him because of [the fact that] for a considerable time he had astonished them with the magic-arts.

**12.** But when they believed Philippos proclaiming-good-news concerning the kingdom of God and the name of Iesous Christ, they were being immersed, both men and women.

**13.** And Simon himself also believed, and having been immersed, was continuing-with Philippos; and beholding signs and great powers taking-place, he was amazed.

**14.** And the apostles, the [ones] in Hierosolyma, having heard that Samareia has received the word of God, sent to them Petros and Ioannes;

**15.** who, having come down, prayed concerning them so that they might receive [the] Holy Spirit;

**16.** for not-yet was it having fallen upon any of them, but only having-been-immersed they were-existing into the name of the Lord Iesous.

**17.** Then they were laying the hands upon them, and they were receiving [the] Holy Spirit.

**18.** And Simon, having seen that through the laying-on of the hands of the apostles the Spirit is given, offered to them money,

**19.** saying, "Give also to me this authority, so-that upon whomever I may lay the hands, he may receive [the] Holy Spirit."

**20.** But Petros said to him, "The silver of you with you may-it-be unto destruction, because the gift of God you supposed to acquire through money.

**21.** There is not to you a part nor lot in this word, for the heart of you is not straight before God.

**22.** Repent therefore from this wickedness of you, and petition the Lord, if indeed the intent of the heart of you shall be forgiven to you;

**23.** for into a gall of bitterness and a bond of unrighteousness I see you being."

**24.** And answering, Simon said, "Petition you on behalf of me to the Lord, so-that nothing may come upon me of what you have spoken."

**25.** The [ones] therefore, having solemnly-testified and having spoken the word of the Lord, were returning into Hierosolyma, and many villages of the Samaritans they were proclaiming-good-news.

**26.** And an angel of [the] Lord spoke to Philippos, saying, "Rise and go toward the south, upon the way going-down from Hierosolyma into Gaza." This is a desert [place].

**27.** And having risen, he went. And behold, a man, an Aithiops, a eunuch, a powerful-one of Kandake the queen of [the] Aithiopians, who was over all the treasury of her, who had come to worship in Hierosolyma,

**28.** and was returning and sitting upon his chariot, and was reading the prophet Esaias.

**29.** And the Spirit said to Philippos, "Approach and join-yourself to this chariot."

**30.** And having run-toward, Philippos heard him reading Esaias the prophet, and said, "Do you indeed know what you are reading?"

**31.** And he said, "For how should I be able, unless someone shall guide me?" And he called-upon Philippos, having come up, to sit with him.

**32.** And the passage of the Scripture which he was reading was this: "As a sheep to slaughter he was led, and as a lamb before the [one] shearing it [is] voiceless, so he does not open the mouth of him.

**33.** In the humiliation of him the judgment of him was taken-away; the generation of him who shall describe? For the life of him is taken-away from the earth."

**34.** And answering, the eunuch said to Philippos, "I ask you, concerning whom does the prophet say this? Concerning himself, or concerning some other?"

**35.** And Philippos, having opened the mouth of him, and having begun from this Scripture, proclaimed-good-news to him [about] Iesous.

**36.** And as they were going along the way, they came upon some water, and the eunuch says, "Behold, water! What hinders me to be immersed?"

**37.** [This verse is absent from Codex Sinaiticus.]

**38.** And he commanded the chariot to stand-still, and both went down into the water, both Philippos and the eunuch, and he immersed him.

**39.** And when they came up out of the water, [the] Spirit of [the] Lord snatched-away Philippos, and the eunuch saw him no longer, for he was going on his way rejoicing.

**40.** And Philippos was found in Azotos; and passing-through, he was proclaiming-good-news [to] all the cities, until he came into Kaisareia.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations (Codex Sinaiticus, pages 234-235)

1. **Verse 1 -- ΣΑΥΛΟΣ ΔΕ ΗΝ ΣΥΝΕΥΔΟΚΩΝ:** Sinaiticus reads ΗΝ ΣΥΝΕΥΔΟΚΩΝ (was consenting) with the periphrastic imperfect, indicating Saul's ongoing approval of the killing. The word ΑΝΑΙΡΕΣΕΙ (killing/destruction) is used, not a softer term. This is the same word used for judicial execution.

2. **Verse 5 -- "the city of Samareia":** Sinaiticus reads ΤΗΝ ΠΟΛΙΝ ΤΗΣ ΣΑΜΑΡΕΙΑΣ ("the city of Samareia") with the definite article, suggesting a specific city rather than the region generally. Some later manuscripts read "a city" (without the article). The Sinaiticus reading points to the capital city Samaria (Sebaste).

3. **Verse 7 -- Syntactical irregularity:** The Greek construction here is anacoluthic. The subject shifts from "unclean spirits" crying out to "they were coming out." I have preserved this rough construction rather than smoothing it. The nominative participle ΠΟΛΛΟΙ does not agree smoothly with the genitive ΤΩΝ ΕΧΟΝΤΩΝ, reflecting Koine colloquial syntax.

4. **Verse 10 -- "the Power of God, the one being called Great":** Sinaiticus reads Η ΔΥΝΑΜΙΣ ΤΟΥ ΘΕΟΥ Η ΚΑΛΟΥΜΕΝΗ ΜΕΓΑΛΗ. This is a technical title -- "the Great Power" -- possibly reflecting Samaritan theological terminology for a divine emanation or hypostasis.

5. **Verse 16 -- "into the name":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΙΣ ΤΟ ΟΝΟΜΑ (into the name), not ΕΝ ΤΩ ΟΝΟΜΑΤΙ (in the name). The preposition ΕΙΣ with accusative is preserved literally. This distinction matters theologically and I have not harmonized it.

6. **Verse 18 -- Sinaiticus reads ΤΟ ΠΝΕΥΜΑ:** Sinaiticus reads simply "the Spirit" without "Holy" (ΑΓΙΟΝ) in this verse, unlike some later manuscripts that add ΑΓΙΟΝ to harmonize with the surrounding context.

7. **Verse 20 -- ΕΙΗ:** The optative mood ΕΙΗ (may-it-be) is preserved. This is a maledictory or imprecatory optative -- Peter is pronouncing a curse-wish, not merely predicting. Rendered "may-it-be unto destruction."

8. **Verse 24 -- Sinaiticus variant:** Sinaiticus reads ΔΕΗΘΗΤΕ ΥΜΕΙΣ ΥΠΕΡ ΕΜΟΥ ΠΡΟΣ ΤΟΝ ΚΥΡΙΟΝ -- "petition you on behalf of me to the Lord." Some manuscripts add "so that nothing may come upon me of what you have spoken." Sinaiticus includes this clause with ΟΠΩΣ.

9. **Verse 25 -- Direction of evangelization:** The text reads that they were evangelizing many villages of the Samaritans on their return journey to Jerusalem. Sinaiticus supports the reading that the evangelization occurred as they traveled back.

10. **Verse 26 -- ΑΥΤΗ ΕΣΤΙΝ ΕΡΗΜΟΣ:** "This is desert." Ambiguous in the Greek -- it could modify the road or the city Gaza. I have rendered it as a parenthetical remark: "This is a desert [place]." The word ΜΕΣΗΜΒΡΙΑΝ can mean either "south" or "noon/midday." I have rendered it "south" as the directional sense fits the geographic context (Jerusalem to Gaza).

11. **Verse 37 -- Absent in Sinaiticus:** Verse 37 ("And Philip said, 'If you believe with all your heart, it is permitted.' And he answered and said, 'I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.'") is entirely absent from Codex Sinaiticus. This verse is a later interpolation found in some Western manuscripts (notably Codex E and later minuscules). Its absence in Sinaiticus, Vaticanus, Alexandrinus, and P45 confirms it is not part of the earliest text.

12. **Verse 39 -- ΠΝΕΥΜΑ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ:** Sinaiticus reads ΠΝΕΥΜΑ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ("Spirit of [the] Lord") rather than the Byzantine variant ΠΝΕΥΜΑ ΑΓΙΟΝ ΕΠΕΠΕΣΕΝ ΕΠΙ ΤΟΝ ΕΥΝΟΥΧΟΝ ΑΓΓΕΛΟΣ ΔΕ ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ΗΡΠΑΣΕΝ ("the Holy Spirit fell upon the eunuch, and an angel of the Lord snatched away"). The shorter Sinaiticus reading, without the angel, is adopted as the earlier text.

13. **General orthographic notes:** Sinaiticus exhibits its characteristic scriptio continua (no word spacing) with occasional supralinear corrections visible on both pages. The nomina sacra abbreviations are present throughout: ΘΥ for ΘΕΟΥ, ΚΥ for ΚΥΡΙΟΥ, ΠΝΑ for ΠΝΕΥΜΑ, ΙΗΥ for ΙΗΣΟΥ, ΧΥ for ΧΡΙΣΤΟΥ, etc.

14. **Verb tense preservation:** Throughout this chapter, I have maintained the aspectual distinctions of the Greek: imperfects (ΕΚΗΡΥΣΣΕΝ -- was proclaiming, continuous action), aorists (ΚΑΤΗΛΘΕΝ -- having gone down, punctiliar), and perfects where they occur. The periphrastic constructions (ΗΝ ΣΥΝΕΥΔΟΚΩΝ, v.1; ΗΣΑΝ ΒΕΒΑΠΤΙΣΜΕΝΟΙ, v.16) are rendered with their full force.

-- EMET
